trending_styles: Organic/Liquid UI (Interfaces that appear to melt, morph, or flow)
Abstract Experimental UIs (Prioritizing visual experience over pure functionality)
trending_colors: Deep Contrast Modes (Pitch black backgrounds contrasted with single, vivid neon/electric accents)
Earthy/Biophilic Palettes (Muted moss greens, terracotta, and off-whites, signaling 'eco-design')
High-Contrast Monochromatic with single bold pops (Using black/white with one highly saturated color for emphasis)
trending_layouts: Asymmetric and Broken Grids (Breaking linear patterns for visual rhythm)
Atomic Content Hubs (Deep-linked, focused pages replacing the need for a traditional 'Home' page)
Oversized Type Blocks (Using typography as the primary structural element)
Layered and Z-Indexed Depth (Creating visual complexity by layering various non-aligned elements)
trending_elements: Custom Shader Visualizations (Using advanced WebGL/Three.js for complex, non-standard graphical elements)
Animated/Kinetic Text Fields (Type that animates, morphs, or drives the page rhythm)
Curated Aspect Ratio Systems (Using specific, non-square ratios to define flow and rhythm)
Interactive Data Visualization Widgets (Embedding complex, customizable data representations)
trending_interactions: View Transitions API Implementations (Smooth, cinematic page morphing rather than hard page loads)
Scroll-Driven Storytelling (Using complex GSAP/scroll triggers to guide the narrative flow)
Meaningful Friction (Intentional, playful interactive hurdles that force user engagement, resisting 'seamless' UX)
avoid_cliche: Generic 'Gradient Mesh' Hero Backgrounds (Especially purple-to-blue transitions)
Traditional 3-Column Feature Grids with Icons (Predictable, basic, and forgettable structure)
The Standard Homepage Landing Page (Over-reliance on a single, monolithic 'front door' experience)
trends.design_direction: The current trend is moving away from 'frictionless' simplicity toward 'meaningful complexity.' Design must feel intelligent, highly technical, and emotionally engaging, often prioritizing immersive visual spectacle over basic, straightforward usability.
